is a [provincial highway](/wiki/Provincial_highway "Provincial highway") located in the [Montérégie](/wiki/Mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie "Montérégie") region of [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec") south of [Montreal](/wiki/Montreal "Montreal"). It runs from Montée Guay in Saint-Bernard-de-Lacolle and ends in La Prairie at the junctions of [Route 104](/wiki/Quebec_Route_104 "Quebec Route 104") and [Autoroute 30](/wiki/Quebec_Autoroute_30 "Quebec Autoroute 30"). It serves in part as a service route for [Autoroute 15](/wiki/Quebec_Autoroute_15 "Quebec Autoroute 15") until Saint-Philippe and, where it runs along the [Saint-Jacques River](/wiki/Saint-Jacques_River "Saint-Jacques River"), and serves as a service route for [Autoroute 30](/wiki/Quebec_Autoroute_30 "Quebec Autoroute 30") until La Prairie and its northeastern terminus at [Route 104](/wiki/Quebec_Route_104 "Quebec Route 104").
Maisel, Albert Q. *Aliens Are Swarming Over Our Unguarded Northern Border*, *[Look Magazine](/wiki/Look_(American_magazine) "Look (American magazine)")*, New York, November 17, 1953, p. 75.

Former Agricultural Inspection Border Station. ["193 Meridian Road, Champlain, NY, 12919 - Agricultural Property - Off-Market on"](http://www.loopnet.com/Listing/16323817/193-Meridian-Road-Champlain-NY). Loopnet.com. Retrieved 2011-03-02.

| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | ←  [R-216](/wiki/Quebec_Route_216 "Quebec Route 216") |  | →  [R-218](/wiki/Quebec_Route_218 "Quebec Route 218") | | |

**Route 217** is a [provincial highway](/wiki/Provincial_highway "Provincial highway") located in the [Montérégie](/wiki/Mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie "Montérégie") region of [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec") south of [Montreal](/wiki/Montreal "Montreal"). It runs from Montée Guay in Saint-Bernard-de-Lacolle and ends in La Prairie at the junctions of [Route 104](/wiki/Quebec_Route_104 "Quebec Route 104") and [Autoroute 30](/wiki/Quebec_Autoroute_30 "Quebec Autoroute 30"). It serves in part as a service route for [Autoroute 15](/wiki/Quebec_Autoroute_15 "Quebec Autoroute 15") until Saint-Philippe and, where it runs along the [Saint-Jacques River](/wiki/Saint-Jacques_River "Saint-Jacques River"), and serves as a service route for [Autoroute 30](/wiki/Quebec_Autoroute_30 "Quebec Autoroute 30") until La Prairie and its northeastern terminus at [Route 104](/wiki/Quebec_Route_104 "Quebec Route 104").

Route 217 previously extended south from Montée Guay to the [Canada–US border](/wiki/Canada%E2%80%93United_States_border "Canada–United States border"), connecting with Meridian Road at [Champlain, New York](/wiki/Champlain_(town),_New_York "Champlain (town), New York"). However, this port of entry closed around 1950, with the Canada border station later dismantled and the US border station converted into offices for the [United States Department of Agriculture](/wiki/United_States_Department_of_Agriculture "United States Department of Agriculture").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) This 1.2 km (1 mi) segment remained part of Route 217 (and its pre-1970s predecessor, Route 9A) as recent as 2005,[[1]](#cite_note-DR2005-1) but in recent years has been truncated at Montée Guay.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
